steps.label: The Analogy
steps.body: Running a GPU 24/7 for a model that gets 10 requests per hour is like keeping a helicopter running on the helipad all day because you might need it. Land it (stop the instance), and call it when you need it.

steps.label: How It Works
steps.body:
Cost levers for AI on Linode:

- **Quantization** — run 4-bit models (GPTQ, AWQ) to fit larger models on smaller GPUs
- **Right-sizing** — match GPU plan to model size (7B fits 1 GPU, 70B needs 4)
- **Batch inference** — process requests in batches instead of one-at-a-time
- **CPU inference** — use Neural Magic's sparse models on CPU Linodes for lighter workloads
- **Scheduled scaling** — stop GPU instances during off-hours via API or cron
- **Flat pricing** — Linode's hourly billing with no egress surprises

steps.label: Real World
steps.body: A quantized 7B model (AWQ 4-bit) runs comfortably on a single RTX 4000 Ada (16GB VRAM). That's ~$1.50/hour vs. ~$6/hour for a 4-GPU setup running the full-precision 70B model. For low-traffic APIs, script linode-cli to spin up GPU instances on demand and shut them down after idle periods.
"Quantize models to fit smaller GPUs, use CPU inference for light workloads, stop instances when idle. Linode's flat pricing means no egress surprises eating your AI budget."

quiz.question: A 7B parameter model is too slow on CPU. What's the cheapest GPU option?

options.explain: 4 GPUs is massive overkill for a 7B model.

options.text: 1× RTX 4000 Ada with a 4-bit quantized model (AWQ/GPTQ)
options.explain: A quantized 7B model fits in ~4GB VRAM. One 16GB GPU is more than enough.

options.text: A Dedicated CPU with more RAM
options.explain: More RAM doesn't help — you need GPU acceleration for acceptable latency.
EdgeWorkers run lightweight JS, not ML inference.

quiz.question: Your GPU inference API gets traffic only during US business hours. How do you cut costs?

options.text: Keep it running — the hourly rate doesn't change
options.explain: Hourly billing means you pay for idle time. Shut it down to stop charges.

options.text: Script linode-cli to stop the instance at 7pm and start it at 7am
options.explain: You only pay when the instance is running. Automate start/stop with cron or CI/CD.

options.text: Move to a smaller plan at night
options.explain: GPU plans can't be resized live. Stop/start is simpler and more effective.
Linode doesn't have a reserved instance model — it's all on-demand hourly.
